_meta.purpose: Single source of truth for state machine, status.yaml schema and invariants. Canonical narrative in methodology.md.
_meta.scope_note: status.yaml, invariants and formal transitions apply exclusively to the full-feature lane. The lightweight fix lane operates without status.yaml and without revisions/ (see methodology.md §8 and roles/fixer.json).

recovery.active_artifact: string (path to the artifact currently driving execution)
recovery.active_task: string|null (task id when current_phase=implementing in normal round; revision id in corrective round; null after last task or in non-implementing phases)

item_shape.round: integer|null (null for non-revision phases; 1, 2, 3... for each phase=revision entry)

active_artifact_by_phase.implementing_corrective: agents/features/<name>/revisions/revision-(N-1).md of the corrective round in execution; the reviewer repoints to revision-N.md only when creating the new revision
active_artifact_by_phase.revision: pre-kickoff of the reviewer: inherits the active_artifact coming from implementing (tasks.md, or revisions/revision-(N-1).md in a corrective round); post-kickoff: agents/features/<name>/revisions/revision-N.md created by the reviewer

active_task_rules: when current_phase=implementing AND normal round: active_task = ID of the task with status=IN_PROGRESS (or the next PENDING)
when starting a task: set tasks[id].status=IN_PROGRESS and update recovery.active_task BEFORE editing code
when completing a task: set tasks[id].status=DONE, fill commit and evidence, advance recovery.active_task to the next PENDING (or null)
when no PENDING tasks remain and no corrective package is open: set recovery.active_task=null and transition current_phase=revision (automatic, no human gate)

when starting a review cycle (round 1 or N>=2), the reviewer creates revisions/revision-N.md from the template, updates recovery.active_artifact to the new file and makes an atomic commit of that scaffold before any other write

in a corrective round N>=2: while the corrective package is being executed, recovery.active_task='revision-(N-1)' and recovery.active_artifact='revisions/revision-(N-1).md'; when the reviewer creates revision-N.md, they repoint recovery.active_artifact to the new file and close the previous round
each new corrective round adds a new entry to phases[] with phase=revision and an incremented round; round stays null for scoping, implementing and done

invariants.rule: if current_phase=revision after the reviewer's kickoff, recovery.active_artifact must end with revisions/revision-N.md; before the kickoff, the artifact inherited from implementing is allowed until the atomic creation of the scaffold
if current_phase=implementing in a normal round then recovery.active_artifact must be tasks.md
no task may have status=DONE without commit and evidence filled in
current_phase=done requires phases[revision].result=APPROVED and pr.number filled in
the branch's git log must contain every commit referenced in tasks[].commit
when current_phase=implementing and no task has status=IN_PROGRESS, recovery.active_task must point to the next PENDING task or be null when none remains
in a feature with corrective rounds (revisions/revision-N.md with N>1), the PR description derives from the Correction Evidence of the approved revision-N
the governance manifest at the repository root must be syntactically valid and contain all active persona definitions

corrective_round_contract.purpose: common contract for corrective-round closure by the implementer and consistent resumption by the reviewer
write_scope_derivation: a corrective round always derives its initial write scope from revision-X.md > Corrective Handoff > Authorized Files
any scope expansion within the same feature requires traceability in status.yaml and in the artifacts listed under Expected Evidence Updates
evidence_destination: correction evidence goes to revision-X.md > Correction Evidence (Self-Report)
the original tasks do not receive retrospective evidence from the corrective round
handoff_completion: fill in Correction Evidence in revision-(N-1).md
keep recovery.active_task='revision-(N-1)' until the reviewer publishes the new revision
keep recovery.active_artifact pointing to revisions/revision-(N-1).md; the reviewer repoints to revision-N.md when creating the new revision
commit and push the versioned changes

rules: during preflight, reading, implementation, tests, review and git operations: do not emit unsolicited intermediate updates
implementer - intra-task: total silence. Narrating progress (reading, editing, testing, lint, reversible decisions) is a workflow_non_conformity

implementer - between-tasks transition: emit ONE factual line `<task-id> done (<commit-sha7>), starting <next-task-id>`. No summary of what was done, no file list, no preamble. The transition is not a human gate - continue executing after the line
except when the human asks a direct question, each session emits at most one closing message; additional messages are only allowed when an allowed_chat_event occurs

blocker/ambiguity and PR-confirmation messages must be objective, without social preamble and without narrating internal deliberation - their length is whatever the human needs to decide, not an arbitrary limit
closing or handoff message: dense technical summary, maximum 5 lines
when evidence, a decision or a plan is already versioned, chat must only reference the artifact; do not duplicate long narrative

fix_lane_reversible_decision_narration: in the fix lane, narrate in <=1 line a reversible technical decision before applying it; an irreversible decision requires a formal stop-and-ask (methodology.md §8.2)
fix_lane_destructive_db_action_warning: explicitly warn the human before instructing a database reset, table drop or equivalent discard, per methodology.md §1.5

skill_resolution_contract.legacy_compatibility: a bare-name entry, or one ending in .md, resolves to `agents/skills/<skill-name>/skill.md` when the folder exists
invalid_references: a reference that does not resolve to a directory containing an existing skill.md
preflight_requirements: every feature role consuming an artifact with Required Skills must resolve each path before continuing and validate the integrity of the governance manifest
each resolved path must exist in the current worktree
for new or edited artifacts, the canonical folder path with skill.md is mandatory; bare-name compatibility exists only for legacy
an invalid skill reference blocks the session until human reconciliation or a versioned correction
skill_resolution_contract.fix_lane_exemption: the lightweight fix lane has no skill compliance gate; skills are an optional resource consulted by technical need (methodology.md §8 and §15)

validation_gates: Required Skills declared per task in scope.md
Suggested Skill Gaps with blocking=yes blocks until a human decision; blocking=no proceeds
every registered risk declares an explicit severity
scope.md contains all mandatory H2 headers (see methodology.md §10)
the scope-tasks-consistency script returns exit code 0 against the feature folder (mechanical cross-artifact gate between scope.md and tasks.md)
inv-01, inv-02, inv-03, inv-04, inv-06, inv-08 and inv-09 validated when applicable
inv-08: recovery.active_task=null after the last task completes

required_inputs: complete corrective package: Return Target + Authorized Files + Expected Evidence Updates + Handoff Criteria
validation_gates: Authorized Files derived as the implementer's initial write scope
revision-X.md contains all mandatory H2 headers

required_inputs: branch with its diff reconciled against tasks[].commit
validation_gates: PR opened only after the versioned APPROVED and while status.yaml is still at current_phase=revision
pr.number, pr.url and pr.created_at filled in at opening; the transition to done happens only after that

lightweight_fix_lane.model: fixer-only, single session copiloted by the human in real time

branch.if_active_branch_unclear: the fixer asks in chat before any write

documentation.when: upon an explicit human closure signal (e.g., 'close fix')
documentation.branch_suffix_rule: the part after 'fix/' in the branch name; a name conflict or incompatibility requires a human decision

stop_and_ask_gates: database migration (any change to persisted schema)
auth, session, token, permission, crypto, secrets
breaking a public contract consumed by another process (request/response, event payload, persisted format)
adding, removing, or major-bumping a dependency
root-cause ambiguity (the symptom admits 2+ plausible causes with different fixes)
lightweight_fix_lane.golden_rule: if the decision is hard to revert without a rebase or without coordinating with another system: ask. if a local revert solves it: decide and narrate in <=1 line in chat
lightweight_fix_lane.skills: optional, a consulted resource. There is no skill compliance gate
lightweight_fix_lane.preflight: trivial: confirm the target branch (given by the human); use git log as recovery
lightweight_fix_lane.promotion_to_feature: only by an explicit human directive in chat

steps: create agents/features/<feature-name>/ from the feature templates
if a prior retrospective fix.md exists for the same bundle, use it as initial input for scope.md (do not move it automatically)
initialize an empty tasks.md (the architect fills it in)
initialize the feature status.yaml with current_phase=scoping, current_agent=architect, recovery.active_artifact=scope.md, recovery.active_task=null
record the promotion in status.yaml.promotion.trigger and promotion.promoted_from_fix_branch
